2606.29503.title: The Verbose Context Problem in Medical Records
2606.29503.paper_summary: The verbose context problem occurs when structured concepts have token-inefficient textual representations. This bottleneck is acute in population health: cohort-level analysis of longitudinal patient records requires reasoning over thousands of medically-coded events, often exceeding 400K tokens in total. We present PopMedQA, a benchmark isolating this problem through computational tasks on groups of longitudinal patient records. We construct the benchmark using neopatient, a new library for language-controlled generation of artificial patient records. Through extensive ablations -- including prompting strategies, prompt compression, and agentic decomposition -- we find that domain-independent methods fail to alleviate the verbose context problem. There remains significant opportunity to exploit domain-specific structure in language model inputs for population-scale reasoning.
2606.29503.paper_summary_zh: 冗長上下文問題發生在結構化概念具有令牌效率低下的文本表示時。這一瓶頸在公共健康領域尤為明顯：對於縱向患者記錄的隊列級分析需要對數千個醫療編碼事件進行推理，通常總令牌數超過400K。我們提出了PopMedQA，這是一個通過對縱向患者記錄組進行計算任務來孤立這一問題的基準。我們使用neopatient構建基準，這是一個用於語言控制生成人工患者記錄的新庫。通過廣泛的消融實驗——包括提示策略、提示壓縮和代理分解——我們發現領域無關的方法無法緩解冗長上下文問題。在語言模型輸入中利用特定領域結構以進行人口規模推理仍然存在重大機會。
2606.29503.author: Shiva Kaul et.al.
2606.29503.authors: Shiva Kaul, Min-Gyu Kim, Anjum Khurshid, Sriram Vishwanath
